economic_notes: Nation owns/controls 5 business enterprises: Sunchild Construction GP Ltd., Sunchild Gravel GP Ltd., Sunchild Store (2005) Ltd., Sunchild Oil & Gas Ltd., and Sunchild Utilities Limited Partnership. These are accounted for using the modified equity method. Trust funds held for Agricultural Benefits and Treaty Land Entitlement claims litigation.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 but audit was signed December 12, 2022 — over 18 months after year-end, which is unusual. The statement of financial activities shows a surplus before tangible capital assets of $1,958,037 but a net deficit of $4,310,376 after capital acquisitions of $6,268,413. Five GBEs (Sunchild Construction, Sunchild Gravel, Sunchild Store, Sunchild Oil & Gas, Sunchild Utilities LP) accounted for by modified equity method and NOT fully consolidated. Sunchild Store (2005) Ltd. is listed in the notes but no financial data is provided for it. A $1,533,331 promissory note to Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations for Treaty Land Entitlement claims is interest-free until settlement. COVID-19 note is described as a 'subsequent event' suggesting this may be the fiscal year 2020-2021 filing done very late. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + Health Canada + ISET + Trust Funds Capital + FNDF + School EPP + Covid-19 + AB Gov programs and other government-sourced lines; osr derived as total revenue minus government transfers. expenses_education includes School Operations, Aboriginal Headstart, Contributions, O&M, Skills Link, EPP, Covid-19, Bussing. expenses_housing includes Band Owned Housing Administration and Housing. expenses_social includes Social Services, Social Development Service Delivery Admin, Community Wellbeing Initiatives, Child Welfare, Child and Family Services. annual_debt_service is demand loan repayment of $200,000 only; promissory note has no repayment this year.
